page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 56 vCPUs mapped to 28 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 224 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with both stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. Passmark memory performance is highly rated in the top 10%, supported by a 224 MB L3 cache. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core results falling in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth for large, uncached blocks also registers in the middle 50%, contrasting with the top-tier cached small-block read speeds.

The absence of bundled local storage represents a key architectural tradeoff, requiring reliance on external network-attached storage. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high physical core density and strong multi-threaded performance offer a solid resource-to-compute ratio for parallelizable workloads without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. This hardware profile is appropriate for multi-threaded applications, database operations, ray tracing, image processing, and medium-scale LLM text generation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 56 vCPUs and 224 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. With 28 physical cores and a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it delivers strong multi-core performance, placing in the top 25% for multi-threaded benchmarks, and top 10% for Passmark memory tests. However, single-core performance and uncached large-block memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, representing a tradeoff that requires external storage solutions. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallel workloads, making it suitable for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, ray tracing, and medium-scale language model text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 is a general-purpose instance with 56 vCPUs, 224 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong multi-core performance and top-tier memory scores.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-56 features 56 vCPUs, 224 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, offering balanced general-purpose compute performance.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring fifty-six virtual cores, strong multi-threaded performance, and top-tier memory benchmark results.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 56 vCPUs and 28 physical cores
Configured with 224 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio
Strong multi-core and top-tier memory benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Suited for database operations, ray tracing, and multi-threaded workloads
